note: References curated from Pinterest/web, Pinterest/OS, Cosmos/UX. Benchmark not yet run. Reference image URLs marked TODO where only board URLs are currently available.

sets.name: Editorial
sets.prompt: Design a landing page for an editorial fashion magazine. Typography-forward, photography as the primary visual, generous whitespace, dark or warm off-white palette.
sets.brief: Landing page for a fashion editorial magazine. Strong type hierarchy, photography as primary visual element, airy layout. Not a blog, not a startup — a magazine site.

tasteDirection.typography: Editorial serif or refined sans headings, tight tracking on display text
tasteDirection.palette: Near-black or warm off-white background, minimal accent

successNotes: Photography fills sections rather than decorating them
Airy spacing that reads as intentional, not empty
Minimal UI chrome — no nav clutter
knownRisks: Photography underused — shrunk into cards instead of filling sections
Wrong type weight — too light or too regular

references.title: Telescope — scattered editorial collage hero
references.reason: Scattered image collage with editorial photography on warm off-white. Shows playful composition with restrained palette.

references.title: Glass Effect — editorial poster / visual manifesto
references.reason: Strong grid typography, red/black/white, editorial poster logic. Sets the typographic discipline ceiling.

references.title: Maison Margiela — editorial website concept
references.reason: Black/white editorial website, photography-forward, serif display type, strong image/text relationship.

references.title: &Co agency — oversized headline hero
references.reason: Oversized editorial headline with photography hero, warm cream palette, editorial structure with team section.

references.title: Nike Circularity — grid editorial
references.reason: Grid-based editorial layout, black/white, structured sections with strong typographic hierarchy.

references.title: Merigrove — photography-forward editorial
references.reason: Full-bleed photography hero, editorial type scale, cream/black palette, oversized display type.

references.title: Fashion editorial — dark/light magazine splits
references.reason: Magazine-style photography grid with dark/light split layouts. Shows editorial pacing and image treatment.

references.title: Photography studio — minimal editorial grid
references.reason: Minimal grid portfolio with warm lighting and editorial structure. Shows restrained navigation and image-first layout.

references.title: Architecture firm — full-bleed editorial hero
references.reason: Full-bleed photography hero with editorial typography, light/dark section contrast, strong hierarchy.

references.title: PSL Arch — full editorial website
references.reason: Complete editorial website with gray palette, grid layout, serif headings, awards section, blog grid. Shows full-page editorial structure.

sets.name: Clean SaaS
sets.prompt: Design a landing page for a B2B SaaS productivity tool. Clean light background, clear value proposition above the fold, feature sections, single strong CTA.
sets.brief: B2B SaaS landing page. White or light gray base, geometric sans, product UI mockup as hero, feature cards, single primary CTA. Not generic startup — closer to Linear or Stripe.

tasteDirection.typography: Geometric sans throughout, tight hierarchy, no serif
tasteDirection.palette: White or light gray background, single blue or neutral accent

successNotes: Clear value proposition in hero without jargon
Feature sections that show the product, not just describe it
CTA that feels earned, not shouted
knownRisks: Generic startup hero with gradient and floating UI elements
Default SaaS visual language with no distinctive quality

references.title: monefy.ai — Finance tracker made for your business
references.reason: Dark gradient SaaS hero with strong product framing. Clear value prop, trial CTA. High-quality execution of the fintech SaaS pattern.

references.title: We eliminate repetitive tasks so your team can focus on what matters
references.reason: Clean white SaaS with workflow diagram. Shows minimal product-only aesthetic with geometric UI diagram instead of illustration.

references.title: AI deserves the same security as software
references.reason: Light SaaS landing, blue accent, device mockup as hero. Clean type hierarchy, concise section structure.

references.title: Automate Reordering and Supplier Decisions
references.reason: Clean workflow SaaS with two-column feature layout. Minimal chrome, structured proof sections.

references.title: Agency Landing Pages — 959 Marketing Examples
references.reason: Diverse collection of high-quality landing pages. Useful for calibrating what strong SaaS landing page diversity looks like across the set.

sets.name: Creative Agency
sets.prompt: Design a landing page for an independent creative or branding agency. Bold headline, strong typographic hierarchy, clear point of view, services listed without looking corporate.
sets.brief: Creative agency landing page. Oversized display headline, black or white base with one bold accent, editorial grid, asymmetric layout. Not corporate — authored and confident.

tasteDirection.typography: Oversized display headline, editorial grid, strong weight contrast between heading and body
tasteDirection.palette: Black or white base with one bold accent — orange, red, or electric blue

successNotes: Distinct visual point of view — could only be this studio
Headline carries enough weight to be memorable
Services clear without being a feature list
Photography or work samples used with authority
knownRisks: Palette too safe — beige or standard blue

references.title: The Clab
references.reason: Strong creative agency presence. Sets the bar for what a high-taste studio site should feel like.

references.title: &Co agency redesign — Jane Vi
references.reason: Agency redesign case study. Shows how typographic hierarchy and personality can define a studio identity.

references.title: Weiden Haus — We Make You See
references.reason: Black/white typographic editorial. Shows how strong type alone can carry an agency identity without any decoration.

references.title: Stop paying AI companies to train your replacement
references.reason: Bold orange/black editorial layout with grid and oversized headline. Shows how attitude and typographic confidence combine on the same page.

references.title: NOISE magazine — orange/street photography
references.reason: Street photography plus bold magazine typesetting. Adds cultural texture — shows how image and type coexist with attitude.

sets.name: Brutalist / Typographic
sets.prompt: Design a landing page where typography is the primary layout element. Extreme hierarchy, raw grid, one or two colors maximum.
sets.brief: Typographic brutalist landing page. Heavy display type at oversized scale, black/white + one accent max, grid structure visible or implied. Typography IS the layout — not a background for content.

tasteDirection.typography: Heavy display typeface, oversized scale, type fills sections rather than sitting in them
tasteDirection.palette: Black and white plus maximum one accent — red, yellow, or deep blue
tasteDirection.density: Typography as density — no empty sections, but no clutter either

successNotes: Type scale that feels intentional at every breakpoint
Grid structure holds even without decoration
One accent color used sparingly and with purpose
Page feels like a poster that also works as a website
knownRisks: Raw model adds decoration to fill what it reads as empty space
Gradient or soft palette creeps in
Type scale collapses to normal web sizes
Becomes unreadable rather than deliberately challenging

references.title: Typografie Schweizer — bold red/black Swiss
references.reason: Swiss typography system, red/black, grid-dominant. High-signal reference for structural typographic layout at its most rigorous.

references.title: Swiss Style magazine layout
references.reason: Clean Swiss grid, black/white, strong column structure. Shows how Swiss typographic discipline translates to layout systems.

references.title: The Submerged Sonata Vol.2 — Friedrichson Berlin
references.reason: Berlin editorial poster, oversized date/title typography, monochrome. Pure type-as-layout reference — numbers and text become the visual.

references.title: Spring Order — Tokyo/Western typography collision
references.reason: Black background, blue/gold/white type collision between Japanese and Western type systems. Adds cultural texture and shows how multiple type systems can coexist with structural discipline.

references.title: Cavix — black/gold vintage display
references.reason: Black background, bold vintage display type, grid-structured. Demonstrates how historical display type reads as brutalist when used at full scale.

gaps.osBoard: Pinterest/OS board is mostly generative art and graphic patterns — not suitable for web generation layout benchmarks. Use for mood/texture references only.
gaps.imageUrlsNeeded: All references marked imageUrl: TODO need stable image URLs captured before the benchmark can be run reproducibly.
gaps.nextPriority: Luxury set needs 3-5 dedicated luxury brand website references before it can be added.
